q.en: Who is the narrator, or the 'I' telling the story?
q.zh: 谁是故事的叙述者，也就是那个讲述故事的'我'？

options.en: Kevin
Bobby
Derek
The theater manager

explain.en: The story is told from Kevin's point of view. He describes sneaking into the movie, watching his friends, and how he felt at the end.
explain.zh: 故事是以 Kevin 的视角讲述的。他描述了自己溜进电影院、看着朋友们，以及结尾时的感受。

q.en: Which movie did the boys watch first at the theater?
q.zh: 男孩们在影院最先看的是哪部电影？

options.en: Attack on Venus
Rodent Extreme
The Serpent King
Monster Trucks

explain.en: They watched Attack on Venus first (the movie with the four-headed serpent). Then Bobby suggested sneaking into Rodent Extreme.
explain.zh: 他们最先看的是 Attack on Venus（那部有四头蛇怪的电影）。之后 Bobby 提议溜进 Rodent Extreme。

q.en: What kind of monster was in the first movie the boys watched?
q.zh: 男孩们看的第一部电影里是什么样的怪物？

options.en: A four-headed serpent
A surfing mouse
A giant spider
A monster truck

explain.en: The book says they could 'almost feel the hot breath of the terrible four-headed serpent' in the first movie.
explain.zh: 书中说他们几乎能'感觉到那头可怕的四头蛇怪的热气'。

q.en: What does the word 'gloated' mean as it is used in the story?
q.zh: 故事里使用的 'gloated'（幸灾乐祸）一词是什么意思？

options.en: To show in a rude way that one is pleased by another's misfortune
options.zh: 以粗鲁的方式表现出对别人不幸的幸灾乐祸

options.en: To whisper quietly so no one can hear
options.zh: 小声耳语不让别人听见

options.en: To feel sorry for someone
To laugh happily with friends

explain.en: The glossary defines 'gloated' as showing in a rude or selfish way that one was pleased by another's misfortune. Bobby gloated when Kenny got caught.
explain.zh: 词汇表把 'gloated' 定义为以粗鲁或自私的方式表现出对他人不幸的幸灾乐祸。Bobby 在 Kenny 被抓时幸灾乐祸。

q.en: Why does the author compare Derek's eyes to 'saucers' when the manager's light appears?
q.zh: 当经理的手电光照来时，作者为什么把 Derek 的眼睛比作'碟子'？

options.en: To show how frightened and surprised Derek was
options.zh: 为了表现出 Derek 有多害怕和惊讶

options.en: To show that Derek was sleepy
options.zh: 为了表现 Derek 很困

options.en: To describe the shape of his glasses
options.zh: 为了描写他眼镜的形状

options.en: To show that Derek was hungry
options.zh: 为了表现 Derek 饿了

explain.en: The author uses the simile 'his eyes were as wide as saucers' to show Derek's fear when the manager's flashlight searched the theater.
explain.zh: 作者用'他的眼睛瞪得像碟子一样大'这个比喻，来表现经理手电光扫过影院时 Derek 的恐惧。

q.en: Why did Kevin first say no to going to see Rodent Extreme?
q.zh: Kevin 一开始为什么拒绝去看 Rodent Extreme？

options.en: He had spent his money on the first movie and popcorn
options.zh: 他已经把钱花在第一部电影和爆米花上了

options.en: He did not like movies about mice
options.zh: 他不喜欢关于老鼠的电影

options.en: His mother told him to come home
Bobby was mean to him

explain.en: Kevin checked his pocket and said, 'No, I'm broke,' because after one movie and popcorn he was running low on money.
explain.zh: Kevin 摸了摸口袋说'不，我没钱了'，因为在看了一部电影又买了爆米花后，他的钱所剩无几。

q.en: How did the boys get into Rodent Extreme without buying tickets?
q.zh: 男孩们没有买票是如何进入 Rodent Extreme 的？

options.en: Bobby led them through a door so they could sneak in unseen
options.zh: Bobby 带他们从一扇门溜进去，不被人发现

options.en: They bought tickets at the counter
Kenny paid for everyone
The manager let them in for free

explain.en: Bobby said they were already inside the complex and he 'sneak[s] into other movies all the time.' He showed them 'the good door to go through so nobody would see us.'
explain.zh: Bobby 说他们已经在大楼里了，而且他'经常溜进别的电影'。他带他们走'不会被发现的门'。

q.en: What is the main lesson, or focus question, of this story?
q.zh: 这个故事的主要寓意（聚焦问题）是什么？

options.en: Following the crowd is not always a good idea
options.zh: 随大流并不总是个好主意

options.en: Movies are always better with friends
options.zh: 和朋友一起看电影总是更好

options.en: Sneaking into movies is a smart trick
options.zh: 溜进电影院是个聪明的把戏

options.en: Bobby is the coolest kid in school
options.zh: Bobby 是学校里最酷的孩子

explain.en: The book's Focus Question asks, 'Why is following the crowd not always a good idea?' Kevin learns this when he follows Bobby and gets Kenny in trouble.
explain.zh: 书的聚焦问题是'为什么随大流并不总是个好主意？'Kevin 在跟着 Bobby 并害 Kenny 惹上麻烦后明白了这一点。

q.en: How did Kevin feel about Bobby by the end of the story?
q.zh: 到故事结尾时，Kevin 对 Bobby 是什么感觉？

options.en: He no longer thought Bobby was so cool
options.zh: 他不再觉得 Bobby 那么酷了

options.en: He wanted to be Bobby's best friend
options.zh: 他想成为 Bobby 最好的朋友

options.en: He was angry at Kenny instead
options.zh: 他反而对 Kenny 生气

options.en: He was glad Bobby got caught
options.zh: 他很高兴 Bobby 被抓了

explain.en: After Kenny got caught and Bobby gloated, Kevin thought, 'To tell the truth, I didn't think Bobby was so cool anymore, either.'
explain.zh: 在 Kenny 被抓、Bobby 幸灾乐祸之后，Kevin 想：'说实话，我也不觉得 Bobby 那么酷了。'

q.en: Why was Kenny taken out of the theater by the manager?
q.zh: Kenny 为什么被经理带出了影院？

options.en: He could not show a ticket because he had sneaked in
options.zh: 他溜进影院所以拿不出票

options.en: He was talking too loudly
He ate too much popcorn
Bobby reported him to the manager

explain.en: The manager asked to see Kenny's ticket; Kenny said he lost it, but really he had sneaked in without one, so the manager took him out.
explain.zh: 经理要求看 Kenny 的票；Kenny 说他弄丢了，但其实他是没票溜进来的，所以经理把他带走了。

prompt.en: If you were Kevin's friend, what advice would you give him about doing what he knows is right when his friends are doing something wrong?
prompt.zh: 如果你是 Kevin 的朋友，当朋友们在做不对的事时，你会给他什么建议，让他去做自己认为正确的事？

en: Kevin followed Bobby mainly because he 'didn't want to be left out' (p. 9).
Bobby did not care that Kenny got into big trouble (p. 13).
By the end, Kevin realized following the crowd wasn't really 'cool.'
A good answer mentions speaking up or choosing not to join.
zh: Kevin 跟着 Bobby 主要是因为他'不想被冷落'（第9页）。
Bobby 根本不在乎 Kenny 惹上了大麻烦（第13页）。
到结尾时，Kevin 意识到随大流其实并不'酷'。
好的回答会提到说出自己的想法，或选择不跟着做。
